frontier: Improve the direction review surface, the highest-stakes decision point in the loop
goal: Make the direction review moment legible and deliberate by showing what documents will change and providing clear context before approval

tasks.description: Restructure DirectionView with clear document preview headers and rationale organization
tasks.instructions:
Rewrite src/modules/main-panel/direction.view.tsx to:

1. Create a header section showing:
   - 'Direction Proposal' title
   - Brief description of what the user is approving ("This updates your project direction and conventions")
   - Visual indicator of included documents (direction.md, conventions.md, and any other docs in the proposal)

2. Reorganize the content into sections:
   - Rationale section (the current pre-block, but with a clear label)
   - Document previews section showing:
     * Each document name with a small badge/label
     * A read-only preview of the first 5-7 lines of each document
     * A subtle button or link to "view full document"

3. Style guidelines:
   - Use semantic color for the header: 'Direction Proposal' in --foreground, subtitle in --foreground-muted
   - Document badges should use --surface-raised background with --border
   - Previews should use --code-bg for the code areas (matching the diff viewer theme)
   - Keep the existing button layout at the bottom (Approve/Discard) but style them consistently

4. Keep all existing logic (approveDirection, discardDirection, acting, error states) unchanged

Context: The goal is to make the user understand at a glance what documents they're approving and what will change, turning a raw dump into an oriented, deliberate decision.

tasks.description: Extract a reusable DocumentPreview component for displaying document snippets with view-full links
tasks.instructions:
Create src/components/document-preview.tsx to:

1. Accept props:
   - name: string (e.g., 'direction.md')
   - preview: string (first 5-7 lines of the document)
   - fullContent?: string (full content for the modal, optional)

2. Render:
   - A labeled header: name in --foreground, 'Preview' in --foreground-faint
   - A code-style preview area using --code-bg background
   - Truncation indicator if content is truncated ("... (view full document)")
   - On click, optionally open a modal or navigate to view full (handle in direction.view for now)

3. Style:
   - Use --code-bg for preview area background
   - Use --code-border for subtle border
   - Monospace font for the preview
   - Padding/spacing consistent with globals.css theme

4. Keep it simple — the component is primarily display, not interactive beyond preview text. Interactivity (view full) will be wired by the parent.

tasks.description: Integrate DocumentPreview component into DirectionView and wire document sidebar navigation
tasks.instructions:
Update src/modules/main-panel/direction.view.tsx to:

1. Import the DocumentPreview component

2. Extract preview snippets from direction.docs:
   - For each doc in direction.docs, take the first ~150 characters or first 5-7 lines (whichever is cleaner)
   - Pass name and preview to DocumentPreview

3. Wire the 'view full document' interaction:
   - When user clicks a document preview, call selectView({ kind: 'doc', name: doc.name })
   - Use useMagent() to access selectView
   - This navigates to the existing DocDiffView for full content

4. Ensure:
   - The direction.md (rationale) and conventions.md are always shown
   - Additional docs from direction.docs are listed below
   - All previews are read-only
   - The layout is clean and the user understands at a glance what documents are included

5. Keep error handling and action states as-is
